title: Correcting a Faulty Greatest Common Divisor (GCD) Function

statement.markdown: Write a function to compute the Greatest Common Divisor (GCD) of two integers using the Euclidean Algorithm. However, the function has a logic flaw and returns incorrect results. Your task is to identify and fix the bug.
statement.inputFormat: The function will take two integers as input. The inputs can be positive, negative, or zero.
statement.outputFormat: The GCD of the two integers should be returned as a positive integer.
statement.notes: You should use the Euclidean Algorithm to compute the GCD. The algorithm iteratively replaces 'a' and 'b' with 'b' and 'a mod b' until 'b' is zero. The GCD is then 'a'.

hints.text: Review the Euclidean Algorithm to understand how the GCD is computed.

editorial.title: Correcting a Faulty Greatest Common Divisor (GCD) Function
editorial.intuition: The Euclidean Algorithm iteratively replaces 'a' and 'b' with 'b' and 'a mod b' until 'b' is zero. The GCD is then 'a'.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force approach would be to iterate from 1 to the smaller of 'a' and 'b' and check if it divides both 'a' and 'b' without leaving a remainder.
editorial.betterSolution: The Euclidean Algorithm is an efficient way to compute the GCD.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the Euclidean Algorithm to compute the GCD.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The Euclidean Algorithm is a well-known method for computing the GCD, and its correctness can be proven by induction.
editorial.dryRun: To dry run this problem, you can start with small inputs and manually compute the GCD using the Euclidean Algorithm.

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int gcd(int a, int b) { if (a < b) return b; return gcd(a, abs(a - b)); } };
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution { public int gcd(int a, int b) { if (a < b) return b; return gcd(Math.abs(a - b), b); } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def gcd(self, a: int, b: int) -> int:    if a < b: return b;    return self.gcd(abs(a - b), b);
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function gcd(a, b) { if (a < b) return b; return gcd(Math.abs(a - b), b); }

functionSignature.python: def gcd(self, a: int, b: int) -> int:

solutions.python:
class Solution: def gcd(self, a: int, b: int) -> int:    if b == 0:        return a    else:        return self.gcd(b, a % b);    
with open(0, 'r') as f: inputs = [int(x) for x in f.readline().split()]; print(Solution().gcd(inputs[0], inputs[1]));
solutions.cpp: class Solution { public: int gcd(int a, int b) { if (b == 0) return a; return gcd(b, a % b); } }; int main() { int a, b; cin >> a >> b; cout << gcd(a, b); return 0; }
